label: Day 1 — Morning: Arrivals & Orientation
description: Day 1 is about landing safely. Participants arrive from four different countries after long journeys — the priority is making everyone feel welcomed, oriented, and at ease before any programme content begins. This slot is purely logistical and relational.
objectives: Ensure all participants arrive and are checked in without stress
Distribute all practical information (rooms, schedule, meals, house rules)
Create a first sense of safety and belonging within the group

steps.title: Airport / transfer coordination
steps.body: Facilitators coordinate arrivals in shifts. WhatsApp group already active for real-time updates.

steps.title: Check-in & room assignment
steps.body: Participants receive room keys, welcome pack, and printed daily schedule. Mixed-nationality rooms encouraged.

steps.title: Venue tour
steps.body: Short walk around the venue — dining area, meeting room, garden, bathrooms, emergency exits.

steps.title: Practical briefing
steps.body: Meal times, curfew, wifi, emergency contacts, house rules. Keep it short and visual — use the printed one-pager.

steps.title: Free time before dinner
steps.body: Participants settle in, unpack, rest. No structured activity — first informal conversations happen naturally.

facilitatorNote: Don't rush this slot. The quality of arrival sets the emotional tone for the entire exchange. If some groups are delayed, adapt — never start a formal session with half the group missing.
